title: coryphopteris engleriana
intro: Coryphopteris engleriana, also known as Engler's fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Coryphopteris engleriana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically prepared by steeping the leaves or roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
